compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN-REVIEW REMEDIATION (rounds 2-4) + claim. Round-1 BLOCK (G6 threshold mechanism): resolved by aligning to the SHIPPED plan-review precedent â€” thresholds live in a COMMITTED criteria_routing.json read by the registry's threshold_for, NOT in gates/code-review.yaml (WS3's file holds no threshold values). Round-2/3 BLOCKs: added a docs/review-kernel.md 'code-review consumer' section (captures the novel patterns); dropped the 'derived/regenerate-no-diff' overpromise (criteria_routing.json is hand-maintained + test-validated, matching the precedent); removed the false 'WS3's EXISTING overlay_triggers op already reads these globs' claim (the Round-A glob-match trigger LOGIC is WS3's work; WS2 supplies only the glob DATA in criteria_routing.json, single-sourced â€” not duplicated in front-matter). Round-4 -> VERDICT PASS, 0 blocking, signed.

Residual 9 advisories (all non-blocking; baked into implementation or acknowledged):
- A2 (active_triggers): defined as the union of criteria across surviving findings (matches plan-review's pass4_coach); implemented as such.
- A6 (verify re-grounds against diff = measurable): a test asserts code-review-verify.md embeds VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD verbatim + references the diff context var.
- A7 (exact prompt ids): the overlay prompts are code-review-<overlay_id> (e.g. code-review-security); resolve via get_prompt.
- A8 (coach pick failure): kernel coach() returns [] on empty picks; gate-level recovery is WS3's (mirrors plan-review). A9 (separate coach prompt): warranted â€” code moves + code active_triggers differ from plan moves.
- A1/A3/A5/A4 (routing-index duplication concern, glob calibration deferred, file count/thin-slice, AC phrasing): acknowledged; structural-floor nitpicks left intentionally.

compiled_state.description:
# WS2 â€” Overlay catalog + code-review prompts + move-catalog

## Why
The base reviewer (WS1) escalates to SPECIALIST overlays; the gate also triggers some overlays deterministically by file globs. This story authors that catalog (the specialist reviewers keyed by WS1's `OVERLAY_IDS`) plus the per-gate kernel plug-ins (verify preamble + Pass-4 code moves + the `threshold_for` posture resolver) the consumer seam requires.

## What
- The overlay catalog: one finder prompt per `OVERLAY_IDS` member (WS1 owns the enum; WS2 adds CONTENT), each a contract-bearing front-matter prompt. Per-overlay `applies_to` globs live in `criteria_routing.json` (the single source), not the prompt front-matter.
- A code-review verify-prompt preamble that EMBEDS `VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD` and re-grounds against the diff.
- A code Pass-4 move-catalog: a `MOVE_REGISTRY_SCHEMA` instance (each move `{name, template, applies_when}`).
- Code-review criteria routing: EXTEND WS1's `code_review/registry.py` (modeled on `plan_review/registry.py`) with a `threshold_for(criteria)` resolver reading a committed `criteria_routing.json`.
- The `code-review-coach` Pass-4 prompt (selects applicable moves; the kernel renders them).

## OVERLAY_IDS (WS1 owns the enum; restated for self-containment)
`security, performance, i18n, a11y, db-migrations, docs, supply-chain, api-compat, iac, tests, llm-prompts` â€” the closed vocabulary from `code_review/registry.py`. WS2 adds one finder prompt + one `criteria_routing.json` entry per id; it never introduces a new id.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-<overlay>.md` (Ã—11) + `code-review-verify.md` + `code-review-coach.md` â€” `category: code-review-pass` (NOT `review`).
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py` â€” EXTEND WS1's module (which already holds `OVERLAY_IDS`): add the criteria-routing reader + `threshold_for` resolver, MODELED ON `plan_review/registry.py` (same shape, code_review package). "Parallel to plan_review/registry.py" = same structure, not a separate file.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` â€” a COMMITTED routing index (the analog of `plan_review/criteria_routing.json`, which is likewise hand-maintained + read by its registry; NOT auto-generated). A test VALIDATES it (every overlay id present, schema well-formed, secrets/security keys present with `blocking_enabled=False`).
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/moves.py` â€” the code move-catalog instance.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/contracts.py` â€” EXTEND WS1's module: register the `code_review_coach` move-pick contract (alongside WS1's `code_review_base_output`).
- `docs/review-kernel.md` â€” EXTEND with a "code-review consumer (b744)" section documenting the criteria-routing reader + `threshold_for`, the committed `criteria_routing.json`, the move-catalog, and the `code-review-pass` category separation (captures the novel patterns â€” the ADR/design-doc the gate requires).
- OUT OF SCOPE: the escalation union + workflow wiring + `gates/code-review.yaml` (WS3); the secrets/security DETECTORS (WS5) â€” WS2 authors the security overlay PROMPT, not the scanner.

## Threshold-loading mechanism (resolves the G6 BLOCK + the T1/T3/E1 advisories)
Follows the SHIPPED plan-review precedent EXACTLY â€” thresholds are NOT in any gate YAML:
- Per-criterion ROUTING (`exec` / `applies_to` / `default_posture` / `block_threshold` / `blocking_enabled`) lives in the COMMITTED `code_review/criteria_routing.json` (analog of `plan_review/criteria_routing.json`).
- `threshold_for(criteria) -> (block_threshold, blocking_enabled)` READS that index (as `plan_review/registry.py` resolves `block_threshold`/`default_posture`). Default for an absent key/field: `(0.95, False)`.
- `gates/code-review.yaml` (WS3) holds NO threshold values â€” it only gates WHICH criteria run via `when:`, like plan-review.yaml's `include_<ID>`.

## Overlay `applies_to` globs (resolves E2/T8/T2 "vocabulary undefined" + the over-broad-glob advisory)
Per-overlay `applies_to` globs are authored ONLY in `criteria_routing.json` (the single source â€” the analog of plan-review's `applies_at` living in its routing index, NOT in prompt front-matter; this avoids the dual-source drift A5 flagged). Overlays with no precise file signal are ESCALATION-ONLY (empty `applies_to` â€” they run only when the base reviewer's `recommend_overlays` escalates to them, never deterministically), avoiding firing on every change:
- `security` â†’ `**/auth*`, `**/*secret*`, `**/security/**`, `**/signing*`, `**/*cred*`
- `performance` â†’ ESCALATION-ONLY (no glob â€” base reviewer escalates; a `**/*.py` glob would fire on nearly every change, A12)
- `i18n` â†’ `**/locale/**`, `**/*i18n*`, `**/translations/**`
- `a11y` â†’ `**/*.html`, `**/*.jsx`, `**/*.tsx`, `**/templates/**`
- `db-migrations` â†’ `**/migrations/**`, `**/alembic/**`, `**/*schema*.sql`
- `docs` â†’ `**/*.md`, `docs/**`, `**/README*`
- `supply-chain` â†’ `**/requirements*.txt`, `**/pyproject.toml`, `**/package*.json`, `**/*.lock`, `**/Pipfile*`
- `api-compat` â†’ `**/*api*`, `**/schemas/**`, `**/*.proto`, `**/cli*`, `**/mcp_server*`
- `iac` â†’ `**/*.tf`, `**/*.tfvars`, `**/k8s/**`, `**/helm/**`, `**/ansible/**`, `**/Dockerfile*`
- `tests` â†’ `**/test_*.py`, `**/*_test.py`, `**/tests/**`
- `llm-prompts` â†’ `**/reviewers/**`, `**/prompts*`, `**/*.prompt`, `**/schemas/*.schema.json`

Calibration of globs + thresholds is DEFERRED. WS2's deliverable is the glob DATA (in `criteria_routing.json`). The deterministic Round-A trigger LOGIC â€” matching the diff's changed files against these globs to emit `include_<overlay>` flags â€” is WS3's work (WS3 decides whether to extend the existing `overlay_triggers` op or add a code-review glob-matcher); WS2 does NOT assert that mechanism already exists.

## Relationship to the existing single-pass reviewers (resolves the T1 overlap advisory)
The new `code-review-security` / `code-review-tests` (etc.) overlays are DISTINCT from the single-pass `security.md` / `tests.md` reviewers: the new ones are `category: code-review-pass` Pass-1 KERNEL finders (claim/criteria/evidence/impact shape, fed to the kernel passes), whereas the legacy reviewers are `category: review` (review_result shape) selected by the single-pass route WS4 retires. They do not collide (different ids, different category, different output shape).

## Move-catalog â€” concrete starter moves + `applies_when` vocabulary (resolves T8/E2)
The move-catalog (`moves.py`) is a `MOVE_REGISTRY_SCHEMA` instance, `validate_move_registry`'d at load. `applies_when` tags are drawn from a CLOSED vocabulary = the `OVERLAY_IDS` plus the literal `"always"`. The gate passes `active_triggers` = the union of `criteria` carried by the SURVIVING findings (exactly as plan-review's `pass4_coach` computes `{c for f in surviving for c in f.get("criteria", [])}`); the kernel `coach()` then offers ONLY moves whose `applies_when` overlaps `active_triggers` (or is empty / `["always"]`), and the LLM picks among that applicable subset. Starter moves:
- `extract-helper` â€” `applies_when: ["always"]` â€” "Extract the duplicated logic in {subject} into a shared helper."
- `add-regression-test` â€” `applies_when: ["tests", "always"]` â€” "Add a regression test covering {subject}."
- `threat-model` â€” `applies_when: ["security"]` â€” "Threat-model the change to {subject} (trust boundary, input validation)."
- `add-migration-guard` â€” `applies_when: ["db-migrations"]` â€” "Guard the migration in {subject} (expand-contract; reversible; backfill-safe)."
- `bound-the-hot-path` â€” `applies_when: ["performance"]` â€” "Bound or memoize the hot path in {subject}."

## Coach prompt output contract + "deterministic render" (resolves T8/F1/E1/A14)
- `code-review-coach` SELECTS among applicable moves and emits move-picks `[{move_id, subject, finding_refs}]` â€” it authors NO prose. A registered `code_review_coach` structured-output contract (in `code_review/contracts.py`) pins that shape (mirrors `plan_review`'s `_pass4_model`).
- "Deterministic render via the kernel" = the kernel `coach(surviving, registry, *, pick, active_triggers)` substitutes `{subject}` into each picked move's `template`; the LLM only picks, the kernel renders.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One contract-bearing front-matter finder prompt per `OVERLAY_IDS` member; all resolve via `get_prompt(<id>)`. Per-overlay `applies_to` globs (the starter set above; `performance` escalation-only) live in `criteria_routing.json` (single source), not the prompt front-matter.
- [ ] A code verify preamble (`code-review-verify.md`) embeds `VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD` and re-grounds against the diff.
- [ ] A code move-catalog (`moves.py`) validates against `MOVE_REGISTRY_SCHEMA` at load (`validate_move_registry`), each move carrying `applies_when` from the closed `{OVERLAY_IDS âˆª "always"}` vocabulary.
- [ ] The code-review registry exposes `threshold_for(criteria) -> (block_threshold, blocking_enabled)` reading `criteria_routing.json` (modeled on `plan_review/registry.py`).
- [ ] `criteria_routing.json` is committed (per-criterion `exec`, `applies_to`, `default_posture`, `block_threshold`, `blocking_enabled`); a test validates every `OVERLAY_IDS` member has an entry and the schema is well-formed.
- [ ] `threshold_for` BEHAVIOR is tested: a default criterion â†’ `(0.95, False)`; the secrets/security keys exist with `blocking_enabled=False`; an unknown criterion â†’ the default.
- [ ] The `code-review-coach` prompt is authored with a registered `code_review_coach` move-pick contract; a test asserts the kernel `coach()` renders a picked move's template deterministically.
- [ ] `docs/review-kernel.md` gains a "code-review consumer (b744)" section documenting the criteria-routing reader + `threshold_for`, the committed `criteria_routing.json`, the move-catalog, and the `code-review-pass` category separation (captures the novel patterns).

## Plan-review remediation (Round-11 + Round-12)
- WS2â†’WS5 threshold handoff: WS2 OWNS `threshold_for` + ALL criterion keys, shipping the secrets + High-Critical-security keys with `blocking_enabled=False`; WS5 flips EXACTLY those two to `True`. Recorded in `criteria_routing.json`; a test asserts the keys exist with `blocking_enabled=False`. Neither story ships a value the other silently assumes.
- `criteria_routing.json` is a COMMITTED, hand-maintained routing index (the plan-review precedent), validated by a test â€” NOT auto-derived by a script (that overpromise is removed).
compiled_state.title: WS2: Overlay catalog + finder/verify/coach prompts + code move-catalog

verify_commands.dd_text: overlay registry + code move-catalog tests pass
